concerns.text: The 'Critical Notes' section mandates implementing 'bond rotation and atom masking' per spec FR-004. This directly contradicts the plan.md (Complexity Tracking) which explicitly forbids these methods as chemically invalid and mandates 'functional-group-preserving edge dropout' instead. The task list creates a blocking conflict by enforcing a method the plan has already rejected as a violation of Constitution VII.

The 'Critical Notes' section instructs to 'flag or impute' missing environmental data per spec FR-002. This contradicts plan.md (Constraints & Assumptions) which mandates **exclusion** of such records to prevent confounding. The task list T014 correctly implements exclusion, but the Critical Notes enforce the spec's flawed imputation logic, creating a semantic weakening of the plan's corrective constraint.

Task T022 implements 'functional-group-preserving edge dropout' but omits the 'SMILES canonicalization' augmentation step required by plan.md (Constitution VII, n ≤ 150 case). The plan explicitly lists both methods as the 'chemically valid augmentation' strategy to meet the ~2x expansion target. Omitting canonicalization risks failing the Small Dataset Robustness principle.

Spec SC-004 requires triggering a 'power analysis warning' if the dataset size is <150. The task list contains no specific task to implement the logic that calculates sample size and generates this warning artifact. This drops an acceptance criterion from the spec into the implementation plan.

Spec SC-005 requires verifying that Integrated Gradients identifies ester bonds in the top [deferred] of attribution scores for ≥90% of hydrolysis cases. The task list lacks a specific verification task to measure this threshold or generate the evidence required to satisfy this success criterion, leaving the criterion unmeasurable.
